name: mol
description: Project workflow toolkit organized around the planner→generator→evaluator harness pattern. 17 shared skills (spec, litrev, impl, fix, refactor, simplify, review, debug, test, ship, web, docs, note, commit, push, pr, tag) + 14 single-axis agents (architect, tester, scientist, compute-scientist, optimizer, documenter, undergrad, pm, ci-guard, web-design, security-reviewer, janitor, reviewer, playwright-evaluator) split into producer/reviewer kinds per docs/agent-design.md. /mol:review is the unified static reviewer — supports --axis=<name> (arch/perf/docs/ux/api/science/numerics/visual/security/hygiene) for single-dimension scoping and aggregates via the reviewer agent. /mol:simplify is the write-mode applier for janitor's hygiene findings. Git workflow chain: /mol:pr → /mol:push → /mol:commit → /mol:ship gates; follows the standard GitHub fork convention (origin = your fork, upstream = canonical repo) with no extra config — /mol:tag pushes release tags to upstream while /mol:push goes only to origin. Four-zone layering (docs / .agent / .claude / CLAUDE.md) with active-vs-passive split: notes in .agent/, specs in .claude/specs/ with checkbox-tracked Tasks, /mol:impl ticks them and deletes the spec on completion. Adapts per project via mol_project frontmatter (Atomiverse, molpy, molexp, molrs, molvis, molq, molnex). Pair with mol-agent for harness install/maintenance.
